title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver disabled runtime shell design draft body pre-draft decision

necessityProof.consumer: Node v340 pre-draft decision archive verification
necessityProof.whyV338CannotBeReused: Node v338 verifies the v337 body candidate archive, but it intentionally does not decide whether the next step may prepare a body draft under disabled boundaries.
necessityProof.whyThisIsNotBodyDraft: v339 records a pre-draft decision and controls only; it does not write body content, implementation steps, provider/client logic, or runtime behavior.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop before body draft content, credential value, raw endpoint URL, provider/client, HTTP/TCP, Java write, mini-kv write/admin command, or automatic upstream start.

preDraftDecisionRecord.sourceSpan: Node v338 disabled design draft body candidate archive verification

decisionQuestions.question: Why make a pre-draft decision now?
decisionQuestions.answer: Node v338 verified the v337 body candidate archive, so the next useful step is deciding whether a future disabled body draft may be prepared without writing it yet.

decisionQuestions.question: Is the body candidate archive stable enough for a pre-draft decision?
decisionQuestions.answer: Yes. v338 verified route, Markdown, digest, smoke, screenshot, explanation, walkthrough, plan index, and historical fallback evidence.

decisionQuestions.question: Is the pre-draft scope bounded away from implementation?
decisionQuestions.answer: Yes. v339 only records decision questions, preparation controls, and stop conditions; it does not write body content or runtime behavior.

decisionQuestions.question: What must happen before body content is written?
decisionQuestions.answer: Node v340 must verify the v339 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and fallback evidence before any body draft content is written.

decisionQuestions.question: Are runtime side effects still closed?
decisionQuestions.answer: Yes. Credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client instantiation, HTTP/TCP, Java writes, mini-kv write/admin commands, and upstream auto-start all remain false.

preparationControls.control: Reuse the v335 body section catalog instead of inventing new runtime sections.
Do not write body paragraphs, pseudo-code, provider/client details, or runtime steps in v339.
Do not request Java/mini-kv echo unless a later version defines new non-secret handoff fields.
Keep runtime shell implementation, invocation, provider/client, and network surfaces disabled.
Require v340 archive verification before any body draft content is written.
Pause if the next step needs secrets, raw endpoints, network calls, Java writes, or mini-kv write/admin commands.

stopConditions.condition: The next step asks v339 to write actual design body content.
The next step requires reading or rendering credential values.
The next step requires parsing or rendering a raw endpoint URL.
The next step requires instantiating a provider, resolver client, or fake client.
The next step requires HTTP/TCP or managed audit network access.
The next step requires Java SQL, deployment, rollback, ledger, or schema writes.
The next step requires mini-kv LOAD, COMPACT, RESTORE, SETNXEX, or write commands.
The next step requires automatically starting Java, mini-kv, or external services.

warnings.message: v339 only records whether a future disabled design body may be prepared; it does not write the body.

recommendations.message: Use Node v340 to verify the v339 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and historical fallback before drafting any body content.

nextActions: Archive Node v339 as a pre-draft decision before any design body content is written.
Let Node v340 verify the v339 route, Markdown, digest, screenshot, and historical fallback.
Do not request Java or mini-kv echo unless a future version defines new non-secret handoff fields.
Pause before body draft content, credential value, raw endpoint URL, provider/client, HTTP/TCP, Java write, mini-kv write/admin command, or automatic upstream start.
